\\\"2\\\"\\n      uid=2_240 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_241 heading \\\"History\\\" level=\\\"2\\\"\\n    uid=2_242 StaticText \\\"Further information: \\\"\\n    uid=2_243 link \\\"Estimates of uid=2_245 StaticText \\\" and \\\"\\n    uid=2_246 link \\\"Human history\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_history\\\"\\n      uid=2_247 StaticText \\\"Human history\\\"\\n    uid=2_248 description=\\\"Modernity\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Modernity\\\"\\n      uid=2_252 StaticText \\\"modernity\\\"\\n    uid=2_253 StaticText \\\", possible only since the \\\"\\n    uid=2_254 link \\\"Age of Discovery\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Age_of_Discovery\\\"\\n      uid=2_255 StaticText \\\"Age of Discovery\\\"\\n    uid=2_256 StaticText \\\". Early estimates for the population of the world\\\"\\n    uid=2_257 link \\\"[11]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-11\\\"\\n      uid=2_258 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_259 StaticText \\\"11\\\"\\n      uid=2_260 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_261 StaticText \\\" date to the 17th century: \\\"\\n    uid=2_262 link \\\"William Petty\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Petty\\\"\\n      uid=2_263 StaticText \\\"William Petty\\\"\\n    uid=2_264 StaticText \\\", in 1682, estimated the world population at 320 million (current estimates ranging close to twice this number); by the late 18th century, estimates ranged close to one billion (consistent with current estimates).\\\"\\n    uid=2_265 link \\\"[12]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-12\\\"\\n      uid=2_266 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_267 StaticText \\\"12\\\"\\n      uid=2_268 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_269 StaticText \\\" More refined estimates, broken down by continents, were published in the first half of the 19th century, at 600 million to 1 billion in the early 1800s and 800 million to 1 billion in the 1840s.\\\"\\n    uid=2_270 link \\\"[13]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-13\\\"\\n      uid=2_271 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_272 StaticText \\\"13\\\"\\n      uid=2_273 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_274 StaticText \\\"It is difficult for estimates to be better than rough approximations, as even current population estimates are fraught with uncertainties from 3% to 5%.\\\"\\n    uid=2_275 link \\\"[14]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-Kapitza2-14\\\"\\n      uid=2_276 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_277 StaticText \\\"14\\\"\\n      uid=2_278 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_279 heading \\\"Prehistoric patterns\\\" level=\\\"3\\\"\\n    uid=2_280 StaticText \\\"The history of the world's population involves a great deal of speculation. Before 9000 BC, virtually all humans were hunter-gatherers living in small bands that usually verged on the edge of extinction. Survival depended on the capture of hunted animals; if supply of animals dwindled for any reason, humans would starve; if the human population grew too large the number of surviving animals would shrink, leading to starvation the next year for the hunters (humans had not yet invented the technique of domesticating and herding animals). The hunting cultures sometimes fished, and usually gathered wild seeds and nuts, but they did not plant or grow vegetables or any other crops. One very rough estimate is 8 million hunters lived circa 9000 BC when the first of several \\\"\\\"\\n    uid=2_281 link \\\"agricultural revolutions\\\" description=\\\"Neolithic Revolution\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neolithic_Revolution\\\"\\n      uid=2_282 StaticText \\\"agricultural revolutions\\\"\\n    uid=2_283 StaticText \\\"\\\" occurred in the eastern hemisphere (the dates are very conjectural). Around the year 9000 BC in the Middle East, for reasons unknown, some groups began to domesticate and graze sheep. The practice of cultivating plants was invented independently in three places: in the Middle East and Europe (wheat, 6500–3500 BC), in Southeast Asia (rice, 6800–4000 BC), and in Central America and Peru (corn, about 2500 BC).\\\"\\n    uid=2_284 link \\\"[15]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-15\\\"\\n      uid=2_285 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_286 StaticText \\\"15\\\"\\n      uid=2_287 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_288 StaticText \\\"Agriculture provided a steady food supply that could be stored for a year or longer in order to minimize the risk of famine. Farm production could be expanded by systematic human exertion. The new technology of farming meant that the food supply was proportional to the number of workers who could plant and harvest the crops. Every new pair of hands meant more food for the community, so children were valued in agricultural societies. Later, additional workers found useful work in building irrigation canals and systems that provided a stable water supply for crops, especially in Egypt, Mesopotamia, India, China, Peru and Mexico. The population could now grow because new people paid their own way. (It would take many centuries before the arable land supply became a limiting factor.) However, death rates were high, especially for infants and children, so even with high birth rates growth was slow. The same amount of land could support either 1,000 hunters or 100,000 farmers, and it is easy to see which side ultimately would win a fight for the land. Farmers gathered in permanent villages, and through a process of warfare consolidated into much larger states, including those in China, India, Egypt and Mesopotamia. From 300 to 1400 AD large agricultural states also existed throughout the eastern United States, called the \\\"\\\"\\n    uid=2_289 link \\\"Hopewell tradition\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hopewell_tradition\\\"\\n      uid=2_290 StaticText \\\"Hopewell tradition\\\"\\n    uid=2_291 StaticText \\\"\\\" and \\\"\\\"\\n    uid=2_292 link \\\"Mississippian cultures\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mississippian_cultures\\\"\\n      uid=2_293 StaticText \\\"Mississippian cultures\\\"\\n    uid=2_294 StaticText \\\"\\\". They are most famous as \\\"\\n    uid=2_295 link \\\"Mound Builders\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mound_Builders\\\"\\n      uid=2_296 StaticText \\\"Mound Builders\\\"\\n    uid=2_297 StaticText \\\", but their culture collapsed (for unknown reasons) by 1500. The natives encountered by the English and French were nomadic hunters who supplemented their meat diet with cultivated vegetables.\\\"\\n    uid=2_298 link \\\"[16]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-16\\\"\\n      uid=2_299 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_300 StaticText \\\"16\\\"\\n      uid=2_301 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_302 link \\\"[17]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-17\\\"\\n      uid=2_303 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_304 StaticText \\\"17\\\"\\n      uid=2_305 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_306 heading \\\"Ancient and post-classical history\\\" level=\\\"3\\\"\\n    uid=2_307 StaticText \\\"Main articles: \\\"\\n    uid=2_308 link \\\"Classical demography\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classical_demography\\\"\\n      uid=2_309 StaticText \\\"Classical demography\\\"\\n    uid=2_310 StaticText \\\" and \\\"\\n    uid=2_311 link \\\"Medieval demography\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medieval_demography\\\"\\n      uid=2_312 StaticText \\\"Medieval demography\\\"\\n    uid=2_313 link url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Population_curve.svg\\\"\\n    uid=2_314 StaticText \\\"Graph of human population from 10 000 BC to 2000 \\\"\\n    uid=2_315 link \\\"AD\\\" description=\\\"Anno Domini\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anno_Domini\\\"\\n      uid=2_316 StaticText \\\"AD\\\"\\n    uid=2_317 StaticText \\\". It shows exponential rise in world population that has taken place since the end of the seventeenth century.\\\"\\n    uid=2_318 StaticText \\\"Estimates of the population of the world at the time agriculture emerged in around 10,000 BC have ranged between 1 million and 15 million.\\\"\\n    uid=2_319 link \\\"[18]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-18\\\"\\n      uid=2_320 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_321 StaticText \\\"18\\\"\\n      uid=2_322 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_323 link \\\"[19]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-19\\\"\\n      uid=2_324 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_325 StaticText \\\"19\\\"\\n      uid=2_326 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_327 StaticText \\\" Even earlier, genetic evidence suggests humans may have gone through a population bottleneck of between 1,000 and 10,000 people about 70,000 BC, according to the now largely discredited \\\"\\n    uid=2_328 link \\\"Toba catastrophe theory\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toba_catastrophe_theory\\\"\\n      uid=2_329 StaticText \\\"Toba catastrophe theory\\\"\\n    uid=2_330 StaticText \\\". By contrast, it is estimated that around 50–60 million people lived in the combined eastern and western \\\"\\n    uid=2_331 link \\\"Roman Empire\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_Empire\\\"\\n      uid=2_332 StaticText \\\"Roman Empire\\\"\\n    uid=2_333 StaticText \\\" in the 4th century AD.\\\"\\n    uid=2_334 link \\\"[20]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-20\\\"\\n      uid=2_335 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_336 StaticText \\\"20\\\"\\n      uid=2_337 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_338 StaticText \\\"The \\\"\\n    uid=2_339 link \\\"Plague of Justinian\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plague_of_Justinian\\\"\\n      uid=2_340 StaticText \\\"Plague of Justinian\\\"\\n    uid=2_341 StaticText \\\" caused Europe's population to drop by around 50% between the 6th and 8th centuries AD.\\\"\\n    uid=2_342 link \\\"[21]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-21\\\"\\n      uid=2_343 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n      uid=2_344 StaticText \\\"21\\\"\\n      uid=2_345 StaticText \\\"]\\\"\\n    uid=2_346 StaticText \\\" The population of Europe was more than 70 million in 1340.\\\"\\n    uid=2_347 link \\\"[22]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-22\\\"\\n estimated 443 million to 350–375 million,\\\"\\n    uid=2_352 link \\\"[23]\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_population#cite_note-23\\\"\\n      uid=2_353 StaticText \\\"[\\\"\\n suffering the \\\"\\n    uid=2_360 link \\\"Black Death\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_Death\\\"\\n      uid=2_361 StaticText \\\"Black Death\\\"\\n    uid=2_362 StaticText \\\" \\\"\\n    uid=2_363 link \\\"pandemic\\\" description=\\\"Pandemic\\\" url=\\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pandemic\\\"\\n      uid=2_364 StaticText \\\"pandemic\\\"\\n    uid=2_365 StaticText
